acceptanceCriteria: A new `rigor release-check` command runs deterministic pre-tag findings: clean worktree, package.json and .claude-plugin/plugin.json versions matching the requested release version, a CHANGELOG.md section for that version, the committed dist/rigor.cjs byte-identical to a fresh build, HEAD on the expected branch (default main) and matching an optional expected SHA, and a successful exact-SHA GitHub CI result.

The overall release check cannot report `passed` when the target SHA lacks the required successful GitHub CI check; when the remote check is not explicitly requested that finding is unverifiable and the overall status fails closed.
A version mismatch, a dirty worktree, or a stale/hand-edited dist bundle each produces a failed finding and a non-passing overall status.

docs/release.md defines a release branch and pull-request workflow that reaches main only through a protected PR with required checks, records whether repository rules apply to administrators and any unavoidable admin bypass authority, and gives the exact tag/publish procedure; the release commit reaches main via PR, never a direct push.
README.md and README.ja.md describe the same release procedure and both link to docs/release.md.

test/release.test.ts covers success and failure cases for every finding and for CI success/failure/unverifiable using injected facts, without contacting GitHub unless the remote check is explicitly invoked.
npm run test:all, npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict all pass, and the rebuilt dist/rigor.cjs is committed.
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
